Fungal infection.description: An infection caused by fungi such as dermatophytes or yeast, commonly affecting the skin, nails, or mucous membranes.
Fungal infection.suggested_action: Keep the affected area clean and dry. Use antifungal creams or medications as prescribed by a doctor.

Allergy.description: An immune system response to a foreign substance (allergen) that is typically not harmful, such as pollen, dust, or certain foods.
Allergy.suggested_action: Identify and avoid allergens. Use antihistamines or other prescribed medications. Seek emergency care for severe reactions.

GERD.description: Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease — a chronic condition where stomach acid flows back into the esophagus, causing heartburn and irritation.
GERD.suggested_action: Avoid trigger foods, eat smaller meals, don't lie down after eating. Use antacids or prescribed medications.

Chronic cholestasis.description: A condition where bile flow from the liver is reduced or blocked, leading to accumulation of bile acids in the blood.
Chronic cholestasis.suggested_action: Seek medical evaluation. Treatment depends on the underlying cause and may include medications or surgery.

Drug Reaction.description: An adverse reaction to a medication, which can range from mild skin rashes to severe systemic responses.
Drug Reaction.suggested_action: Stop the suspected medication immediately and consult your doctor. Seek emergency care for severe reactions.

Peptic ulcer diseae.description: Open sores that develop on the inner lining of the stomach or upper small intestine, often caused by H. pylori bacteria or NSAIDs.
Peptic ulcer diseae.suggested_action: Avoid spicy foods, alcohol, and NSAIDs. Take prescribed proton pump inhibitors or antibiotics.

AIDS.description: Acquired Immunodeficiency Syndrome — the most advanced stage of HIV infection, severely weakening the immune system.
AIDS.suggested_action: Seek immediate medical care. Antiretroviral therapy (ART) is essential. Regular monitoring by an infectious disease specialist.

Diabetes.description: A chronic metabolic condition characterized by high blood sugar levels due to insufficient insulin production or insulin resistance.
Diabetes.suggested_action: Monitor blood sugar regularly. Follow a balanced diet, exercise, and take prescribed medications or insulin.

Gastroenteritis.description: Inflammation of the stomach and intestines, typically from viral or bacterial infection, causing vomiting and diarrhea.
Gastroenteritis.suggested_action: Stay hydrated with oral rehydration solutions. Eat bland foods. See a doctor if symptoms are severe or prolonged.

Bronchial Asthma.description: A chronic respiratory condition where airways narrow and swell, producing extra mucus and making breathing difficult.
Bronchial Asthma.suggested_action: Use prescribed inhalers and medications. Avoid triggers. Develop an asthma action plan with your doctor.

Hypertension.description: Persistently elevated blood pressure that increases risk of heart disease, stroke, and kidney problems. Often asymptomatic.
Hypertension.suggested_action: Monitor blood pressure regularly. Adopt a low-sodium diet, exercise, manage stress, and take prescribed medications.

Migraine.description: A neurological condition characterized by intense, debilitating headaches often accompanied by nausea, vomiting, and light sensitivity.
Migraine.suggested_action: Rest in a dark, quiet room. Take prescribed migraine medication early. Track and avoid triggers.

Cervical spondylosis.description: Age-related wear and tear of the spinal discs in the neck (cervical spine), which can cause pain, stiffness, and nerve compression.
Cervical spondylosis.suggested_action: Physical therapy, neck exercises, pain management. Consult an orthopedic specialist for severe cases.

Paralysis (brain hemorrhage).description: Loss of muscle function in part of the body, often caused by bleeding in the brain (hemorrhagic stroke).
Paralysis (brain hemorrhage).suggested_action: This is a medical emergency. Call emergency services immediately. Early treatment is critical for recovery.

Jaundice.description: Yellowing of the skin and eyes caused by elevated bilirubin levels, indicating liver dysfunction or bile duct obstruction.
Jaundice.suggested_action: Seek medical evaluation to determine the underlying cause. Treatment varies based on the root condition.

Malaria.description: A life-threatening mosquito-borne disease caused by Plasmodium parasites, characterized by cyclical fever and chills.
Malaria.suggested_action: Seek urgent medical treatment. Antimalarial drugs are essential. Prevention includes mosquito nets and repellents.

Chicken pox.description: A highly contagious viral infection caused by the varicella-zoster virus, producing an itchy rash with fluid-filled blisters.
Chicken pox.suggested_action: Rest, use calamine lotion for itch relief, take antihistamines. Avoid scratching to prevent scarring. Vaccination is recommended.

Dengue.description: A mosquito-borne viral infection causing high fever, severe joint/muscle pain, and potentially hemorrhagic complications.
Dengue.suggested_action: Seek medical care immediately. Stay hydrated, use acetaminophen for pain. Avoid aspirin and NSAIDs.

Typhoid.description: A bacterial infection caused by Salmonella typhi, spread through contaminated food and water, causing prolonged fever.
Typhoid.suggested_action: Seek medical diagnosis and antibiotic treatment. Maintain strict hygiene. Vaccination is recommended for travelers.

hepatitis A.description: A highly contagious liver infection caused by the hepatitis A virus, usually transmitted through contaminated food or water.
hepatitis A.suggested_action: Rest, maintain hydration, avoid alcohol. Most cases resolve on their own. Vaccination provides prevention.

Hepatitis B.description: A serious liver infection caused by the hepatitis B virus, transmitted through blood and body fluids. Can become chronic.
Hepatitis B.suggested_action: Seek medical evaluation. Antiviral medications may be needed for chronic cases. Vaccination is available.

Hepatitis C.description: A viral infection causing liver inflammation, primarily spread through blood contact. Often chronic and can lead to liver damage.
Hepatitis C.suggested_action: Consult a hepatologist. Modern antiviral treatments can cure most cases. Regular liver function monitoring.

Hepatitis D.description: A liver infection caused by the hepatitis D virus, which only occurs in people already infected with hepatitis B.
Hepatitis D.suggested_action: Seek specialist care. Treatment focuses on managing hepatitis B. Hepatitis B vaccination prevents hepatitis D.

Hepatitis E.description: A liver infection caused by the hepatitis E virus, typically transmitted through contaminated water. Usually self-limiting.
Hepatitis E.suggested_action: Rest, stay hydrated, avoid alcohol. Most cases resolve spontaneously. Pregnant women should seek immediate medical care.

Alcoholic hepatitis.description: Liver inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption, leading to liver cell damage and potential liver failure.
Alcoholic hepatitis.suggested_action: Stop alcohol consumption immediately. Seek medical treatment. Nutritional support and medication may be needed.

Tuberculosis.description: A serious bacterial infection primarily affecting the lungs, caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis. Highly contagious.
Tuberculosis.suggested_action: Seek immediate medical evaluation. TB requires a 6-9 month antibiotic regimen. Notify close contacts.

Common Cold.description: A viral infection of the upper respiratory tract affecting the nose and throat. Usually harmless and resolves within 7-10 days.
Common Cold.suggested_action: Rest, stay hydrated, use OTC cold remedies. See a doctor if symptoms persist beyond 10 days.

Pneumonia.description: An infection that inflames air sacs in one or both lungs, which may fill with fluid or pus. Can be life-threatening.
Pneumonia.suggested_action: Seek medical attention promptly. Antibiotics for bacterial pneumonia. Hospitalization may be required for severe cases.

Dimorphic hemmorhoids(piles).description: Swollen blood vessels in the rectal area that can be internal or external, causing pain, bleeding, and discomfort.
Dimorphic hemmorhoids(piles).suggested_action: Increase fiber intake, stay hydrated, use topical treatments. Consult a doctor for persistent or severe cases.

Heart attack.description: A medical emergency where blood flow to the heart is blocked, causing heart muscle damage. Requires immediate treatment.
Heart attack.suggested_action: Call emergency services immediately. Chew an aspirin if available. Time is critical — every minute counts.

Varicose veins.description: Enlarged, twisted veins usually in the legs, caused by weakened valves and vein walls. Can cause aching and swelling.
Varicose veins.suggested_action: Exercise regularly, elevate legs, wear compression stockings. Consult a vascular specialist for treatment options.

Hypothyroidism.description: A condition where the thyroid gland doesn't produce enough hormones, leading to fatigue, weight gain, and cold sensitivity.
Hypothyroidism.suggested_action: Take prescribed thyroid hormone replacement. Regular blood tests to monitor levels. Follow up with an endocrinologist.

Hyperthyroidism.description: A condition where the thyroid gland produces too much thyroid hormone, causing rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and anxiety.
Hyperthyroidism.suggested_action: Consult an endocrinologist. Treatment options include medications, radioactive iodine, or surgery.

Hypoglycemia.description: Abnormally low blood sugar levels that can cause shakiness, confusion, and in severe cases, loss of consciousness.
Hypoglycemia.suggested_action: Consume fast-acting sugar immediately (juice, candy). Monitor blood sugar levels. Consult your doctor to adjust medications.

Osteoarthristis.description: A degenerative joint disease where cartilage breaks down, causing pain, stiffness, and reduced joint mobility.
Osteoarthristis.suggested_action: Stay active with low-impact exercise. Use pain relievers as directed. Physical therapy and weight management help.

Arthritis.description: Inflammation of one or more joints, causing pain, swelling, and stiffness. Includes rheumatoid and other inflammatory types.
Arthritis.suggested_action: Consult a rheumatologist. Treatment may include anti-inflammatory medications, physical therapy, and lifestyle changes.

(vertigo) Paroymsal Positional Vertigo.description: A disorder causing brief episodes of dizziness related to changes in head position, caused by displaced inner ear crystals.
(vertigo) Paroymsal Positional Vertigo.suggested_action: Perform Epley maneuver exercises. Avoid sudden head movements. Consult an ENT specialist if symptoms persist.

Acne.description: A skin condition where hair follicles become clogged with oil and dead skin cells, causing pimples, blackheads, and cysts.
Acne.suggested_action: Maintain a gentle skincare routine. Use prescribed topical or oral medications. Avoid picking at affected areas.

Urinary tract infection.description: An infection in any part of the urinary system — kidneys, ureters, bladder, or urethra. Most common in the bladder.
Urinary tract infection.suggested_action: See a doctor for antibiotics. Drink plenty of water. Seek urgent care if fever develops or pain worsens.

Psoriasis.description: A chronic autoimmune skin condition causing rapid skin cell buildup, resulting in scaling, redness, and inflammation.
Psoriasis.suggested_action: Use prescribed topical treatments, phototherapy, or systemic medications. Moisturize regularly. Manage stress triggers.

Impetigo.description: A highly contagious bacterial skin infection producing red sores that rupture, ooze, and form a yellow-brown crust.
Impetigo.suggested_action: Keep the area clean. Apply prescribed antibiotic ointment. Avoid touching or scratching sores. Practice good hygiene.
